     The Numbers Business Report

War Horse (2011)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $79,883,359Details
International Box Office $76,932,170
Worldwide Box Office $156,815,529
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $30,507,958
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $13,079,593
Total $43,587,551
Further financial details...

Synopsis

The story of a miraculous horse in wartime—a stirring journey that explores a bond of friendship, loyalty and courage. Within the tale of a boy and the feisty colt he never stops believing in, there are sweeping battles, desperate escapes and an odyssey through a world at war. But no matter where they go or what they experience both boy and horse keep forging ahead, driven by devotion and the hope of returning home.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$7,515,402 (9.4% of total gross)
Legs:5.54 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:50.9% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$70,000,000 (worldwide box office is 2.2 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,376 opening theaters/2,856 max. theaters, 6.3 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $120,232,385

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: December 25th, 2011 (Wide) by Walt Disney
International Releases: January 12th, 2012 (Wide) (New Zealand)
February 16th, 2012 (Wide) (Peru)
April 24th, 2025 (Limited) (New Zealand)
Video Release: April 3rd, 2012 by Buena Vista Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for intense sequences of war violence.
(Rating bulletin 2184, 8/10/2011)
Running Time: 146 minutes
Award nomination: 2012 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Cast, crew, or production detail: Animal Lead, Ensemble
Plot point: Addiction, Class Warfare, Dysfunctional Family, Orphan, War
Social setting: World War I
Subgenre: War Drama
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: DreamWorks Pictures, Reliance BIG Entertainment, Amblin Entertainment, The Kennedy/Marshall Company
Production Countries: United States, United Kingdom
Languages: English

Synopsis

The story of a miraculous horse in wartime—a stirring journey that explores a bond of friendship, loyalty and courage. Within the tale of a boy and the feisty colt he never stops believing in, there are sweeping battles, desperate escapes and an odyssey through a world at war. But no matter where they go or what they experience both boy and horse keep forging ahead, driven by devotion and the hope of returning home.
